executiveSummary.text: Autolus is a clinical biotech name where the upside exists only if the science and regulatory path work. At $1.49 with a market cap of roughly $362M and negative EPS, the stock is still deep in speculative territory. This is a pipeline bet, not an operating company. Bottom line: REDUCE - SPECULATIVE . Real upside if clinical outcomes land, but still too binary to rate more positively from here.

businessModel.text: Autolus develops engineered T-cell therapies in oncology. Value comes from clinical trial success, regulatory progress and partnership credibility. Like all biotech development names, the business model is essentially capital deployment into data generation until something works.

financialSnapshot.text: Live price: $1.49 Market cap: $362M Day range: $1.41 - $1.50 P/E: N/A EPS: -$1.08 Beta: 2.10

recentCatalysts.text: Clinical and regulatory updates remain the whole story. Biotech funding conditions matter heavily at this scale. Any data disappointment would be painful from here.

thesisEvaluation.text: Bull (25%): positive clinical and regulatory progress rerates the shares sharply. Base (45%): continued uncertainty keeps the stock speculative and volatile. Bear (30%): data or financing disappointments drive further downside. Weighted conviction: 48/100.

keyRisks.text: Clinical risk: always number one. Funding risk: biotech balance sheets matter. Binary volatility: event-driven drawdowns can be severe.

risks.risk: Funding risk: biotech balance sheets matter.
Binary volatility: event-driven drawdowns can be severe.

whoShouldOwn.text: Should own: only biotech investors comfortable with binary risk. Should avoid: anyone needing earnings visibility or risk control.

recommendation.text: REDUCE - SPECULATIVE at $1.49. Still a data trade, not a de-risked investment case.

entryExit.text: Below $1.20: more interesting for biotech specialists only $1.2-$1.6: speculative zone Key trigger: clearly positive clinical and regulatory progress Conviction Trend Latest conviction: 48/100 .
